title: PS-68864 - Sync Site Capture Upload Requests status back to mobile
body:
PS-68864 - Sync Site Capture Upload Requests status back to mobile

This pull request introduces a new API endpoint and supporting infrastructure to allow mobile clients to retrieve the processing/upload status of Site Capture uploads, and updates mobile capabilities to reflect this new functionality. It also removes legacy or deprecated capabilities and cleans up related packaging and manifest files.

**Site Capture Upload Status API and Data Model:**

- Added a new API endpoint `SiteCaptureProcessingState` to `SyncController` in `ProjectSight.WebAPI` for retrieving Site Capture processing/upload states for a given portfolio and project. This endpoint is intended for use by mobile clients version 2.45.2 and newer.
- Implemented the method `GetUploadProcessingStates` in `SiteCaptureUploadRequestSystem` to fetch upload statuses from the database, mapping database status codes to a new `MobileSiteCaptureState` model. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-642973ff8302f3fcb265e7509a387993f6fd99aa011532933265947f15ea7002R170-R234) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d58278c555baec18bd6dfc26792785e89b11abc1634a2d6d6f4d67c6aa163edbR1-R25)
- Added a new SQL stored procedure `s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates` to return Site Capture upload records (from both active and archive tables) filtered by portfolio, project, and last sync time.

**Mobile Capability Updates:**

- Introduced a new capability `SyncUploadStatus` in `MobileCapability`, and removed/commented out several legacy capabilities (`WorkflowTemplates`, `Support360`, `Announce360`, `SiteCapture`, `Names50`) that are now assumed to be supported everywhere or deprecated.
- Updated `MobileSyncPrep` to advertise the new `SyncUploadStatus` capability to mobile clients instead of the removed legacy capabilities.
- Simplified logic in `SyncSystem` so that `WorkflowTemplates` is no longer marked as unsupported, reflecting the assumption that all servers now support templates.

**Packaging and Manifest Cleanup:**

- Removed `ProjectSight.WebAPI` and its related files from the `Packer.manifest`, reflecting that these files are no longer required in the deployment package for `ServicesAPI`. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-1f4b56eb0421be330f3b0cc5742a3db392f9c4c50c8338579ba7fed479b422ecL5-L13) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-1f4b56eb0421be330f3b0cc5742a3db392f9c4c50c8338579ba7fed479b422ecL78) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-1f4b56eb0421be330f3b0cc5742a3db392f9c4c50c8338579ba7fed479b422ecL110-L114)
- Cleaned up the `StartServices.ps1` manifest by removing a reference to `Overrides\MobileService-Web.config`.
author: rroesler

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds a new mobile-facing API endpoint that returns Site Capture upload/processing states so that mobile clients (v2.45.2+) can sync upload progress back from the server. Also refreshes the `MobileCapability` enum (introducing `SyncUploadStatus`, retiring several legacy flags) and removes `ProjectSight.WebAPI` artifacts from the `ServicesAPI` packaging manifest.

**Changes:**
- New `SiteCaptureProcessingState` GET endpoint plus `SiteCaptureUploadRequestSystem.GetUploadProcessingStates`, backed by a new `s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates` stored procedure that unions active and archive upload-request tables, mapped through a new `MobileSiteCaptureState` DTO.
- Mobile capability enum: adds `SyncUploadStatus` (and reserves `NextNumber`); removes/comments out `WorkflowTemplates`, `Support360`, `Announce360`, `SiteCapture`, `Names50`, and stops advertising the deprecated ones in `MobileSyncPrep`/`SyncSystem`.
- Packaging cleanup: removes WebAPI binaries/configs from `ServicesAPI/Packer.manifest` and drops `Overrides\MobileService-Web.config` from `Core/DeployScripts/Packer.manifest`.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| Core/SQL/base_procs_ignition.sql | New `s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates` proc unioning active and archive tables. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureUploadRequestSystem.cs | New `GetUploadProcessingStates` method that maps DB statuses to `MobileSiteCaptureState`. |
| Core/Common/Components/Mobile/MobileSiteCaptureState.cs | New DTO + state enum returned to mobile. |
| Core/Common/Components/Mobile/MobileCapability.cs | Adds `SyncUploadStatus` and `NextNumber`; comments out legacy capabilities. |
| Core/Common/Components/Mobile/MobileSyncPrep.cs | Replaces removed legacy capability advertisements with `SyncUploadStatus`. |
| Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/SyncSystem.cs | Stops marking `WorkflowTemplates` as not-supported. |
| Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.WebAPI/Controllers/v1/SyncController.cs | New `SiteCaptureProcessingState` GET endpoint. |
| Core/API Layer/ProjectSight.ServicesAPI/Packer.manifest | Drops WebAPI binaries/configs from ServicesAPI package. |
| Core/DeployScripts/Packer.manifest | Removes obsolete `MobileService-Web.config` override. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated no new comments.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: Two unused `using` directives were added to this file: `Google.Apis.Auth.OAuth2` and `System.Xml.Linq`. Neither namespace is referenced anywhere in the file (verified via search). These look like accidental IDE-suggested imports and should be removed to keep dependencies clean.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body:
Several spelling/grammar issues in the new XML doc and inline comments here:
- `Retrive` → `Retrieve`
- `Statues` → `Statuses`
- The doc comment ends mid-phrase ("Site Capture Processing / Upload States/Statues") and the `<param>`/`<returns>` tags are empty. Consider filling them in to match the style used by other endpoints in this controller.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Other endpoints on this controller (e.g. `CompleteMayaUpload` directly above) accept a `CancellationToken cancellationToken` parameter and forward it to `ConfigureAccessForPortfolioDBUserAsync`. This new endpoint instead passes `cancellationToken: default`, which means client disconnects/cancellations cannot propagate. Consider accepting a `CancellationToken` parameter and passing it through to be consistent with the surrounding code.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `UNION` (without `ALL`) forces SQL Server to do a distinct/sort over the combined result set. Since `SiteCaptureUploadRequests` and `SiteCaptureUploadRequestsArchive` are two separate tables and a given `CaptureGuid` is not expected to exist in both (the archive holds requests that were moved out of the active table), `UNION ALL` would give the same results without the deduplication overhead. Recommend switching to `UNION ALL` here.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Per the SQL guidelines, expressions returning 1/0 in SELECT lists should be cast to `BIT`, otherwise SQL Server infers `INT`. The `Uploaded` column is read into a `bool` (`Global.BoolFromDB`) on the C# side, so wrap the `CASE` expression: `CAST(CASE WHEN CapturedAt IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS BIT) AS Uploaded`. Apply to both branches of the UNION.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Spelling: `syning` → `syncing`, and `suported` → `supported` in the comments below.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Spelling: `sever` → `server` in the existing comment for `WorkflowTemplates` (now commented out).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Removing `Support360`, `Announce360`, and `Names50` from the advertised capability list is a behavioral change for any mobile client that gates features on these flags being present in the sync prep response. The companion comment in `MobileCapability.cs` says these "should be supported on all servers now," but mobile clients that still check `capabilities.includes(Support360)` (etc.) will silently believe the server lacks the capability. Please confirm the minimum supported mobile client version no longer reads these flags before merging — otherwise older mobile builds will regress (e.g. stop offering 360 photo/video uploads).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Yes, I guess there could be some old mobiles that still need these for a while.
review_comments.author: rroesler

review_comments.body: Fixed in 6e8f581 — corrected `Retrive` → `Retrieve` and `Statues` → `Statuses` in the summary, and filled in all `<param>` and `<returns>` tags with meaningful descriptions.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `Support360`, `SiteCapture`, `Announce360`, and `Names50` are already retained in the advertised capabilities list in `MobileSyncPrep.cs` (with a "Keep until April, 2027" comment), so older mobile builds will continue to see those flags. No change needed here.
review_comments.author: Copilot

issue_comments.body: @kcostenbader I addressed the Copilot comments but now I need approved
issue_comments.author: rroesler

@@ -5509,6 +5509,36 @@ AS
    WHERE Status = 0 and Locked = 1
 GO
 
+/****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates ******/
+CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates
+  @PortfolioId      uniqueidentifier,
+  @ProjectId        int,
+  @LastSyncDateTime datetimeoffset
+AS
+  SELECT CaptureGuid as UploadGuid,
+         Status as CaptureStatus,
+         Cast((CASE WHEN CaptureFileLocation IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) AS BIT) as Uploaded,
+         LastModified 
+    FROM SiteCaptureUploadRequests
+   WHERE PortfolioId = @PortfolioId
+     AND ProjectId = @ProjectID
+     AND LastModified >= @LastSyncDateTime
+
+   UNION
+   
+  SELECT CaptureGuid as UploadGuid,
+         Status as CaptureStatus,
+         Cast((CASE WHEN CaptureFileLocation IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) AS BIT) as Uploaded,
+         LastModified 
+    FROM SiteCaptureUploadRequestsArchive 
+   WHERE PortfolioId = @PortfolioId
+     AND ProjectId = @ProjectID
+     AND LastModified >= @LastSyncDateTime;
+
+  -- Consider indexes on PortfolioId, ProjectId, LastModified for both tables if this query is slow
+  -- and it is expected to return a small number of records based on the LastSyncDateTime filter 
+GO
+
 /****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_SiteCaptureUploadRequest_by_MayaCaptureId ******/
 CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_enc_get_SiteCaptureUploadRequest_by_MayaCaptureId
   @MayaCaptureId           nvarchar(36)

@@ -3433,6 +3435,34 @@ public async Task<ProjectSightActionResult> CompleteMayaUpload(Guid portfolioGui
             return new ProjectSightActionResult(Ok(result));
         }
 
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Retrieve Site Capture Processing / Upload Statuses for a given portfolio and project.
+        /// </summary>
+        /// <param name="portfolioGuid">The GUID of the portfolio.</param>
+        /// <param name="projectId">The ID of the project.</param>
+        /// <param name="dtLastSyncDateTime">Only records modified after this timestamp are returned. Defaults to all records if not specified.</param>
+        /// <returns>A list of <see cref="MobileSiteCaptureState"/> describing the current processing/upload status for each Site Capture upload request.</returns>
+        [Route("v{version:apiVersion}/{portfolioGuid:guid}/{projectId:int:min(1)}/sync/siteCaptureProcessingState")]
+        [HttpGet]
+        [Produces(typeof(List<MobileSiteCaptureState>))]
+        public async Task<ProjectSightActionResult> SiteCaptureProcessingState(Guid portfolioGuid, int projectId, DateTimeOffset? dtLastSyncDateTime = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default)
+        {
+            // Used in 2.45.2 and newer 
+
+            await ConfigureAccessForPortfolioDBUserAsync(portfolioGuid, projectId, cancellationToken: cancellationToken);
+
+            var error = new ErrorEncountered();
+
+            var captureSystem = new SiteCaptureUploadRequestSystem();
+
+            var result = captureSystem.GetUploadProcessingStates(RequestedBy, dtLastSyncDateTime ?? DateTimeOffset.MinValue);
+
+            RaiseErrorIfHasMessage(error);
+
+            return new ProjectSightActionResult(Ok(result));
+        }
+
+
         #endregion // SiteCapture
 
         #region HELPERS / OVERRIDES
diff --git a/Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureUploadRequestSystem.cs b/Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureUploadRequestSystem.cs
index 3699fb16a87..b2bffed85df 100644
--- a/Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureUploadRequestSystem.cs	
+++ b/Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/SiteCapture/SiteCaptureUploadRequestSystem.cs	
@@ -1,6 +1,8 @@
 using GCCM.Common.SystemFramework;
+
 using ProjectSight.Common.Components;
 using ProjectSight.Common.Components.ApplicationEvents;
+using ProjectSight.Common.Components.Mobile;
 using ProjectSight.Common.Components.SiteCapture.Interfaces;
 using ProjectSight.DataAccess.SiteCapture.API;
 using System;
@@ -162,6 +164,71 @@ public void CompleteRequestForWebUploader(IRequestedBy requestedBy, int recordId
             }
         }
 
+
+        public List<MobileSiteCaptureState> GetUploadProcessingStates(IRequestedBy requestedBy, DateTimeOffset lastSyncDateTime)
+        {
+            // Exceptions are handled by the caller
+
+            // Comment: perhaps mobile should send a list of GUIDS to the server rather than return all Uploads 
+
+            // @PortfolioId uniqueidentifier,
+            // @ProjectId        int,
+            // @LastSyncDateTime datetimeoffset
+            const string STORED_PROC = "s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates";
+
+            using (var ignitionDB = _dbHelperFactory.NewDBHelperIgnition(requestedBy))
+            {
+                using (var reader = ignitionDB.ExecuteReader(STORED_PROC, requestedBy.OrganizationID, requestedBy.ProjectID, lastSyncDateTime))
+                {
+                    var result = new List<MobileSiteCaptureState>();
+
+                    while (reader.Read())
+                    {
+                        var record = new MobileSiteCaptureState();
+                        record.UploadGuid = Global.GuidFromDB(reader[nameof(MobileSiteCaptureState.UploadGuid)]);
+                        record.LastModified = Global.DateFromDB(reader[nameof(MobileSiteCaptureState.LastModified)]);
+                        record.Uploaded = Global.BoolFromDB(reader[nameof(MobileSiteCaptureState.Uploaded)]);
+                        var status = (SiteCaptureUploadRequest.Statuses)Global.IntFromDB(reader["CaptureStatus"]);
+
+                        switch (status)
+                        {
+                            case SiteCaptureUploadRequest.Statuses.None:
+                                record.UploadState = MobileSiteCaptureState.States.Exists; // if a record exists the Request must exist 
+                                break;
+
+                            case SiteCaptureUploadRequest.Statuses.WaitingForFloorProcessingToComplete:
+                                record.UploadState = MobileSiteCaptureState.States.ProcessingFloorPlan;
+                                break;
+
+                            case SiteCaptureUploadRequest.Statuses.ReadyToCreateCapture:
+                                record.UploadState = MobileSiteCaptureState.States.Waiting;
+                                break;
+
+                            case SiteCaptureUploadRequest.Statuses.CreatingCapture:
+                                record.UploadState = MobileSiteCaptureState.States.ProcessingCapture;
+                                break;
+
+                            case SiteCaptureUploadRequest.Statuses.WaitingForCaptureProcessingToComplete:
+                                record.UploadState = MobileSiteCaptureState.States.ProcessingCapture;
+                                break;
+
+                            case SiteCaptureUploadRequest.Statuses.Failed:
+                                record.UploadState = MobileSiteCaptureState.States.Failed;
+                                break;
+
+                            default:
+                                record.UploadState = MobileSiteCaptureState.States.Exists;
+                                break;
+                        }
+
+                        result.Add(record);
+                    }
+
+                    return result;
+                }
+            }
+        }
+
         #endregion
 
         #region Read

-        Announce360 = 12, // from server, server level -- announce new captures (UploadCaptured)

+        //SiteCapture = 10, // from server, project level -- portfolio / project has enabled SiteCapture feature

 }
diff --git a/Core/Common/Components/Mobile/MobileSiteCaptureState.cs b/Core/Common/Components/Mobile/MobileSiteCaptureState.cs
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000..78076a8fe27
--- /dev/null
+++ b/Core/Common/Components/Mobile/MobileSiteCaptureState.cs
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+﻿using System;
+using System.Collections.Generic;
+using System.Linq;
+using System.Text;
+using System.Threading.Tasks;
+
+namespace ProjectSight.Common.Components.Mobile
+{
+    public class MobileSiteCaptureState
+    {
+        public enum States
+        {
+            Exists = 0,
+            ProcessingFloorPlan = 1,
+            Waiting = 2,
+            ProcessingCapture = 3,
+            Failed = 4
+        }
+
+        public Guid UploadGuid { get; set; }
+        public States UploadState { get; set; }
+        public bool Uploaded { get; set; }
+        public DateTimeOffset LastModified { get; set; }
+    }
+}

                 // MobileCapability.AIDailyReport, // not all servers support ... 
                 // MobileCapability.UploadComplete, // when normal Photos use UploadComplete 
 
+                // Keep until April, 2027
                 MobileCapability.Support360,
+                MobileCapability.SiteCapture,
                 MobileCapability.Announce360,
                 MobileCapability.Names50,
+
+                // Keep until June, 2027
+                MobileCapability.SyncUploadStatus
             };
 
             User = new UserInfo(requestedBy, userinfo);

+/****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates ******/
+CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_enc_get_SiteCaptureProcessingStates
+  @PortfolioId      uniqueidentifier,
+  @ProjectId        int,
+  @LastSyncDateTime datetimeoffset
+AS
+  SELECT CaptureGuid as UploadGuid,
+         Status as CaptureStatus,
+         Cast((CASE WHEN CaptureFileLocation IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) AS BIT) as Uploaded,
+         LastModified 
+    FROM SiteCaptureUploadRequests
+   WHERE PortfolioId = @PortfolioId
+     AND ProjectId = @ProjectID
+     AND LastModified >= @LastSyncDateTime
+
+   UNION
+   
+  SELECT CaptureGuid as UploadGuid,
+         Status as CaptureStatus,
+         Cast((CASE WHEN CaptureFileLocation IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END) AS BIT) as Uploaded,
+         LastModified 
+    FROM SiteCaptureUploadRequestsArchive 
+   WHERE PortfolioId = @PortfolioId
+     AND ProjectId = @ProjectID
+     AND LastModified >= @LastSyncDateTime;
+
+  -- Consider indexes on PortfolioId, ProjectId, LastModified for both tables if this query is slow
+  -- and it is expected to return a small number of records based on the LastSyncDateTime filter 
+GO
+
 /****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_SiteCaptureUploadRequest_by_MayaCaptureId ******/
 CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_enc_get_SiteCaptureUploadRequest_by_MayaCaptureId
   @MayaCaptureId           nvarchar(36)
